habitat: Dense coniferous or mixed forests adjoining open areas; pine forest; birch woodland; and tundra edges
story: An icon of the skies, the Northen Hawk Owl (Surnia ulula) is a species from the Strigidae family. It thrives in Dense coniferous or mixed forests adjoining open areas; pine forest; birch woodland; and tundra edges, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the Northen Hawk Owl is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Northen Hawk Owl helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Northen Hawk Owl, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
